summary.intent: Add unit tests and e2e tests to prevent regressions for a feature adding memory/skill self-improvement config fields and session summary logic.
summary.outcome: Added 3 new Rust unit tests, 2 prompt builder tests, and a Playwright e2e spec. Also fixed a pre-existing bug where `#[derive(Default)]` produced wrong defaults for bool fields that had `#[serde(default = "default_true")]`, bringing the total to 247 passing tests.

repo: Config structs use `#[serde(default)]` at the struct level combined with per-field `#[serde(default = "default_true")]`, but when the entire TOML section is absent, serde falls back to `Default::default()` (from `#[derive(Default)]`), which sets bools to `false` — overriding the per-field defaults.
The fix pattern is to remove `#[derive(Default)]` and implement `Default` manually with the correct values for affected structs (`MemoryEmbeddingConfig`, `SkillsConfig`).
E2E tests for settings pages follow a pattern using RPC mocks defined in helpers.js; new settings pages need corresponding mock entries.
Config validation tests live in `crates/config/src/validate/tests/memory.rs`; prompt builder tests in `crates/agents/src/prompt/tests.rs`.

code.finding: `MemoryEmbeddingConfig` had `#[derive(Default)]` + `#[serde(default = "default_true")]` on bool fields. When the config section is absent from TOML, `Default::default()` is used, setting bools to false instead of true. Fixed by implementing `Default` manually.
`SkillsConfig` had the same `#[derive(Default)]` / `#[serde(default = "default_true")]` mismatch for `enable_self_improvement` and `enabled`. Fixed by implementing `Default` manually.
New e2e spec created for Settings > Memory page, covering render of the Agent Self-Improvement section and RPC round-trip persistence of new config fields.

workflow: Running `toml::from_str("")` in tests is the correct way to exercise config defaults for a fully absent TOML section — it surfaces the `Default` vs serde-default discrepancy.
When a Default derivation conflict is found in one struct, audit sibling structs in the same crate for the same pattern.

friction: The `#[derive(Default)]` + `#[serde(default = "default_true")]` combination is a silent footgun — tests initially failed with unexpected `false` values before the root cause was identified.
Had to trace serde's struct-level vs field-level default resolution rules to understand why `toml::from_str("")` didn't honour per-field defaults.
open_items: The e2e spec for Settings > Memory was written but not yet executed in CI — Playwright test run was not shown in the transcript.
The same `#[derive(Default)]` footgun may exist in other config structs with `#[serde(default = "default_true")]` fields — a broader audit was not performed.
